title: calytrix breviseta subsp. breviseta
intro: Calytrix breviseta subsp. breviseta, also known as the Western Starfruit Bush, is a species of flowering plant in the family Myrtaceae native to Western Australia. The plant is traditionally used by the indigenous people of Western Australia for its medicinal properties, which are attributed to its high content of essential oils and flavonoids. The Western Starfruit Bush has been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it a potential treatment for various health issues, including skin conditions and respiratory infections. It can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed orally to harness its medicinal benefits.
